full_response:
We work in units with c = 1 so that time and distance have the same units.  
Indices “⊕” = Earth (rest frame), “S” = spaceship.

1. Data  
Outbound speed v₁ = 0.90 → γ₁ = 1/√(1−0.9²)=1/√0.19 ≈ 2.294  
Inbound speed v₂ = 0.99 → γ₂ = 1/√(1−0.99²)=1/√0.0199 ≈ 7.089  

Proper‐time recorded on the ship  
τ_total = 5 yr = τ₁ + τ_p + τ₂  
where τ_p = 1 yr is the stay on the planet (ship is at rest there).  
⇒ τ₁ + τ₂ = 4 yr  (1)

2. Earth-frame travel times  
Let t₁ be the Earth time for the outbound leg, t₂ for the inbound leg, and L the one–way distance (Earth frame).

L = v₁ t₁ = v₂ t₂  (2)  
⇒ t₁ = (v₂ / v₁) t₂ = (0.99/0.90) t₂ = 1.10 t₂  (3)

3. Relating proper and coordinate times on each leg  
τ₁ = t₁ / γ₁ ,  τ₂ = t₂ / γ₂

Insert into (1):  
t₁/γ₁ + t₂/γ₂ = 4

Use (3):  
(1.10 t₂)/γ₁ + t₂/γ₂ = 4  
t₂ [1.10/γ₁ + 1/γ₂] = 4

Numerical values  
1.10/γ₁ = 1.10 / 2.294157 ≈ 0.479714  
1/γ₂     = 1 / 7.088812 ≈ 0.141103  
Sum      = 0.620817

Hence  
t₂ = 4 / 0.620817 ≈ 6.447 yr  
t₁ = 1.10 t₂ ≈ 7.091 yr

4. Total Earth-frame duration  
t_total = t₁ + τ_p + t₂  
         ≈ 7.091 yr + 1 yr + 6.447 yr  
         ≈ 14.54 yr

\boxed{t_{\text{Earth}} \;\approx\; 14.5\ \text{years}}

Confidence: 78%
